metadata.title: States PCS: Kerala GK Questions for KPSC Examinations

questions.question: Which river is responsible for forming a major delta near the city of Alappuzha in Kerala?
questions.explanation: The Pamba River creates a significant delta near Alappuzha, supporting the region’s backwaters and agriculture, and is central to the Sabarimala pilgrimage.

questions.question: Which district in Kerala receives the highest average annual rainfall?
questions.explanation: Idukki district, located in the Western Ghats, receives the highest average annual rainfall in Kerala due to its mountainous terrain and dense forests.

questions.question: Which of the following biosphere reserves in Kerala is included in UNESCO’s Man and the Biosphere Programme?
questions.explanation: Agasthyamala Biosphere Reserve, spanning Kerala and Tamil Nadu, is recognized by UNESCO for its biodiversity and endemism.

questions.question: Which forest in Kerala is renowned for the periodic blooming of Neelakurinji flowers every 12 years?
questions.explanation: Eravikulam National Park in the Western Ghats witnesses the rare mass blooming of Neelakurinji (Strobilanthes kunthiana) every 12 years, drawing tourists and botanists.

questions.question: Which bird sanctuary in Kerala is located on the banks of the Periyar River?
questions.explanation: Thattekad Bird Sanctuary, also called Salim Ali Bird Sanctuary, lies along the Periyar River and is home to 300+ bird species, including rare endemics.

questions.question: Which environmental movement in Kerala was launched to oppose the construction of a hydroelectric project in Silent Valley?
questions.explanation: The Save Silent Valley Movement blocked a hydroelectric project, preserving Silent Valley’s unique biodiversity; it led to the creation of Silent Valley National Park.

questions.question: Which type of forest is predominantly found in the Western Ghats region of Kerala?
questions.explanation: Tropical evergreen forests thrive in the Western Ghats of Kerala due to high rainfall, dense canopy, and abundant biodiversity, with many endemic species.

questions.question: Which Ramsar site in Kerala is renowned for its extensive backwaters and estuarine ecosystem?
questions.explanation: Ashtamudi Lake is a Ramsar site in Kerala, noted for its unique estuarine backwaters, biodiversity, and traditional livelihoods like coir-making and fishing.

questions.question: Which is the second-longest river of Kerala?
questions.explanation: Bharathapuzha, Kerala’s second-longest river, suffers heavily from sand mining and pollution, threatening its ecosystem and water flow.
